description: Interactive decision tree to help taxpayers select the correct ITR form for FY 2025-26. Start at node Q1 and follow the path based on user answers.

nodes.questionText: What is your legal status as a taxpayer?
nodes.helpText: Select the category that best describes you. This determines which family of ITR forms is applicable.

options.label: Resident Individual (Indian citizen living in India)
options.helpText: An individual who satisfies the residential conditions u/s 6(1) — present in India for 182+ days in the FY, or 60+ days in FY and 365+ days in preceding 4 FYs

options.label: Non-Resident Individual (NRI / Seafarer / Indian Abroad)
options.helpText: An individual who does NOT satisfy residential conditions u/s 6(1). Includes Resident but Not Ordinarily Resident (RNOR) for some income types.

options.label: Hindu Undivided Family (HUF)
options.helpText: A joint family with a Karta. HUF is a distinct tax entity from individual members.

options.label: Partnership Firm or LLP
options.helpText: A registered partnership firm or Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) under the Partnership Act / LLP Act

options.label: Company (Private, Public, or Foreign)
options.helpText: Any company incorporated under Companies Act 2013 or foreign company with income in India

options.label: Trust, Society, or Institution (Charitable/Religious)
options.helpText: Any person registered u/s 12A/12AB or claiming exemption u/s 11/12

options.label: AOP, BOI, or Artificial Juridical Person
options.helpText: Association of Persons, Body of Individuals, or entities like universities, statutory corporations

nodes.questionText: Is this company claiming exemption under Section 11 or 12 (as a charitable/religious institution)?

options.label: No — regular company (business, investment, etc.)
Yes — company registered u/s 12A/12AB and claiming u/s 11/12

nodes.questionText: Is this AOP/BOI registered as a charitable institution (claiming exemption u/s 11/12)?

options.label: No — ordinary AOP/BOI with business or investment income
Yes — registered u/s 12A/12AB and claiming exemption

nodes.questionText: Do you have business or professional income? (Select Yes if you are self-employed, run a trade/business, or provide professional services)
nodes.helpText: Business income includes trade, shop, manufacturing, trading. Professional income includes CA, doctor, lawyer, architect, engineer, consultant services.

options.label: Yes — and I use a presumptive income scheme (44AD, 44ADA, 44AE, or 44B)
options.helpText: 44AD: Small businesses; 44ADA: Specified professionals; 44AE: Goods vehicle operators; 44B: Shipping business

options.label: Yes — I maintain regular books of accounts (actual profit/loss)
options.helpText: You prepare a Profit & Loss Account and Balance Sheet

options.label: No — I have no business or professional income
options.helpText: Income is only from salary, house property, capital gains, or other sources

nodes.questionText: As an NRI, do you have business or professional income in India?

options.label: Yes — business/professional income in India
options.helpText: Business operated through a branch/agent in India, or professional services rendered in India
options.note: NRIs with business income typically use ITR-3. ITR-4 presumptive schemes are available to NRIs only for limited cases (44AE).

options.label: No — only salary, capital gains, property, or other India-sourced income

nodes.questionText: What type of income does the HUF have?

options.label: Presumptive business income (44AD or 44AE)
Regular business income (maintains books of accounts)
No business income (investments, capital gains, house property, etc.)

nodes.questionText: Does the HUF have any of these disqualifying factors?

options.label: More than 2 house properties
Capital gains of any type (other than LTCG 112A within ₹1.25L)
Foreign income or foreign assets
None of the above

nodes.questionText: Does the HUF have capital gains or more than 1 house property?

options.label: Yes — has capital gains or multiple house properties
No — only house property income (1 property) and other sources
options.note: HUFs cannot file ITR-1. They must use ITR-2 minimum.

nodes.questionText: Do any of these conditions apply to you? (Any YES answer may require a different form)
nodes.helpText: These are disqualifying factors that prevent use of the simpler ITR-4 form.

options.label: I own more than 2 house properties
I have capital gains other than LTCG u/s 112A within ₹1.25 lakh
options.helpText: This includes STCG on equity, LTCG on property, capital losses being carried forward, or LTCG u/s 112A above ₹1.25 lakh

options.label: I am a Director in any company
I held unlisted equity shares at any time during FY 2025-26
I have foreign income, foreign assets, or signing authority in foreign accounts
My total income (all sources) exceeds ₹50 lakh
None of the above — I'm eligible for ITR-4

nodes.questionText: Which of these apply to you? (Select the first that applies)
nodes.helpText: We need to determine if you need ITR-2 or can use the simpler ITR-1

options.label: I am a Director in any company
options.helpText: Even a nominee director of any company disqualifies ITR-1

options.label: I held unlisted equity shares at any point during FY 2025-26
options.helpText: Even holding 1 share in an unlisted company for 1 day disqualifies ITR-1

options.label: I have any capital gains (long-term or short-term) or capital losses
options.helpText: Any amount of capital gain — including LTCG u/s 112A even within the exempt limit

options.label: I own more than 1 house property
I have foreign income or foreign assets
options.helpText: Includes foreign bank accounts, overseas investments, foreign salary, etc.

options.label: My total income exceeds ₹50 lakh
None of the above — I have only salary, 1 house property (or none), and other sources

nodes.explanation: You are a resident individual with simple income sources. ITR-1 (Sahaj) is the correct and simplest form for you.
eligibilitySummary: Total income must not exceed ₹50 lakh
warnings: Confirm you are NOT a director in any company
Confirm you did NOT hold unlisted equity shares at any time in FY 2025-26
Confirm you have NO capital gains of any type
nextSteps: Check Form 26AS and AIS on income tax portal
fy202526Notes: Default regime is NEW tax regime — evaluate old vs new regime before filing
Zero tax up to ₹12,75,000 income (new regime, salaried with standard deduction)

nodes.explanation: You need ITR-2 because you have capital gains, multiple house properties, are a director/shareholder of unlisted company, or have foreign income.
eligibilitySummary: Capital gains (LTCG, STCG, or capital losses)
warnings: If you also have business/professional income (books of accounts), you need ITR-3 instead
If you have presumptive income (44AD/44ADA) with no disqualifying factors, check ITR-4 eligibility
fy202526Notes: LTCG u/s 112A: Taxed at 12.5% above ₹1.25 lakh exemption
Schedule FA mandatory if foreign assets or foreign accounts

nodes.explanation: As an NRI with India-sourced income (salary, property, capital gains, etc.), you must file ITR-2. NRIs cannot use ITR-1.
eligibilitySummary: NRI with house property income in India
NRI with capital gains on Indian assets
warnings: NRE account interest is exempt u/s 10(4) — do not include as taxable income
FCNR account interest is exempt — report in Schedule EI (exempt income)
DTAA benefits may apply depending on the country of residence — consult a tax advisor

nodes.explanation: You need ITR-3 because you have business or professional income (maintaining books of accounts), or you have additional income types that disqualify ITR-4.
eligibilitySummary: Individual or HUF with business/professional income (regular books)
Individual who switched from presumptive (44AD) to regular books
Individual with business income + multiple house properties + capital gains
Partners in partnership firms (for their individual ITR)
warnings: Must prepare Profit & Loss Account and Balance Sheet if turnover > ₹1 Crore (or professional receipts > ₹50L)
Tax audit u/s 44AB may be required
F&O trading loss can be set off against business income but not against salary
fy202526Notes: Employer NPS contribution u/s 80CCD(2) deduction raised to 14% (available in both regimes)

nodes.explanation: You qualify for ITR-4 (Sugam) — the simplified form for presumptive income taxpayers. No need to maintain detailed books of accounts.
eligibilitySummary: Resident Individual or HUF using presumptive income scheme
Section 44AD: Small businesses with turnover ≤ ₹3 Crore (digital-heavy) or ≤ ₹2 Crore (otherwise)
Section 44ADA: Specified professionals with gross receipts ≤ ₹75 lakh
Up to 2 house properties (NEW for FY 2025-26)
LTCG u/s 112A within ₹1.25 lakh (NEW for FY 2025-26)
warnings: If you opt out of presumptive scheme, you cannot re-enter for 5 years (44AD)
If cash transactions exceed 5% of turnover, the higher limit of ₹3 Crore does not apply for 44AD
fy202526Notes: NEW: Mandatory disclosure of cash balance and bank balance in Schedule BP as on March 31, 2026
NEW: Up to 2 house properties allowed in ITR-4
NEW: LTCG u/s 112A up to ₹1.25 lakh can be reported in ITR-4

nodes.explanation: Partnership firms, LLPs, AOPs, BOIs, and similar entities file ITR-5.
warnings: Partners file their individual ITRs separately (ITR-2 or ITR-3) for their personal income including share from firm
Share of profit from a partnership firm is EXEMPT in the hands of partner u/s 10(2A)

nodes.explanation: All companies (domestic and foreign) not claiming exemption u/s 11 must file ITR-6.
eligibilitySummary: Domestic companies (Private, Public, OPC, Section 8, etc.)
Foreign companies with income sourced from India
Excludes companies claiming exemption u/s 11 (those use ITR-7)
warnings: Section 8 companies that are charitable (registered u/s 12A/12AB) may use ITR-7 instead
E-filing of ITR-6 is mandatory — no physical filing
DSC (Digital Signature Certificate) is mandatory for companies

nodes.explanation: Persons required to file under Sections 139(4A), 139(4B), 139(4C), or 139(4D) — including charitable trusts, political parties, research institutions, and universities.
eligibilitySummary: Trusts registered u/s 12A/12AB claiming exemption u/s 11/12
Scientific research associations, news agencies, trade unions u/s 139(4C)
warnings: Trust registration under FCRA may have additional compliance requirements
85% income application rule must be met for exemption u/s 11
Failure to comply with conditions u/s 13 leads to loss of exemption
